title: rubus × acicularis
intro: Rubus × acicularis, also known as thimbleberry, is a hybrid plant species that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae). It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which make it useful in treating wounds, skin irritations, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Rubus × acicularis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and provide antioxidant protection. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and its berries can be consumed fresh or used in jams and preserves to harness its medicinal properties.
